item.title: That’s not racist.
item.content:encoded: This is testimony taken from historical evidence in context. In the really real world, these describe rituals dedicated to Xipe Totec. These. Things. Happened. it passes no judgement, it simply accounts. That’s not how racism works.

The fact that a racist, poorly written work is almost at +300 can only be explained by the fact that it’s not only in Series 1, but in the first 100 of Series 1. I guess people lower their standards that much for this SCP.

These were my exact thoughts. If it were posted today, wouldn't it be summary'd for racism? Maybe that should happen now?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one just aged horribly. The clinical tone here is bad, even by Series 1 standards. The author uses "Tests reveal that…" at least five times, and it gets repetitive. It’s also racist. This section here:

Expert analysis hypothesizes that SCP-034 may be of South American origin, and that it may have been used in Native American rituals. Several accounts from Spanish conquistadors exploring the █████████ region support this hypothesis, with detailed writings on how █████ priests would flay their victims alive with similar knives and wear their skin as a tribute to their gods.

should not be on the SCP Wiki at all. The fact that a racist, poorly written work is almost at +300 can only be explained by the fact that it’s not only in Series 1, but in the first 100 of Series 1. I guess people lower their standards that much for this SCP.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for trying to turn someone into a salmon

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article could not be sourced, and thus is assumed to be non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0). As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: Yay
item.content:encoded: Happy +250!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Necropost extreme here because I see a plot hole that seems to have been missed till now.

Complete physical mimicry would logically include things like tattoos, scars, and injuries.
The infiltrator should have been walking around looking like Dr. X did when found, complete with a flayed arm. Not so stealthy!

This could be fixed with a line about how 034 does not copy injuries inflicted by itself; or more in-depth with a test showing how it uses a "snapshot" image of the victim from the moment just before skin was broken by the knife.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Does this remind anyone else of Your Eternal Reward from TF2?

I like this item; I can see getting a lot of use out of it in a fantasy TTRPG.

Basically my problem with this. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should this be revised to take into account current nomenclature such as GoI instead of "scp related groups"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's for clarification purposes, Nifar.

Also, this one is pretty cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So if person can take any shape of another individual can SCP-034 be used to mimic itself? The change would not appear, but what would occur from it? Can this also be used to mimic 2 different person at the same time? Would it mix them?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, the main thing for me here is that we have no way of knowing that this is the only 034 out there, and that the people who sent this guy doesn't have a way of making more of them. Who knows where their plants are hiding? It could be you. It could be me. It could EVEN be—

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The object itself is okay. Hokey but well executed enough to be acceptable. The hook that there's someone out there that the Foundation doesn't know about actively trying to steal identities and who knows what else, and who was unsuccessful THIS time… that's amazing. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Successfully froze my spine (flaying alive and having one's skin worn is a particular personal horror of mine). Some excellently creepy touches like the victim having to remain alive, and nice uses of censorship. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"proportional the amount of flesh used." < to the amount

"will revert to his original form." < [his] should be [their]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh God, lol, yes!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's for clarification purposes, Nifar.

Also, this one is pretty cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The note at the end made the article for me. My only nitpick though is the redaction in:

Cross-species experiments with SCP-034 have resulted in [DATA EXPUNGED]

Even though that's common practice (especially among older articles), it always got on my nerves… +1 anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does this remind anyone else of Your Eternal Reward from TF2?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how it hints at a possible mole in the Foundation. How else would the "unknown benefactor" know about the sites and the real Dr.'s role (lack of a better word) in the Foundation? +1

item.title: Re: Possible uses.
item.content:encoded: I don't think this would work, since it was mentioned that SCP-034 only worked effectively on human subjects.

item.title: Re: Possible uses.
item.content:encoded: If anyone ever does this (and I think it would be cool), SPC 1177 would be an interesting test subject.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this item; I can see getting a lot of use out of it in a fantasy TTRPG.

Why's it classed as Euclid, though? Seems more like a Safe to me.

item.title: Re: Possible uses.
item.content:encoded:
Except for name who directly mentions this in his post.
Is Able classified as non-human? Just because he is ever-respawning doesn't necessarily make it so that he is non-human. Although it is a good indication. The series canon with the interaction of Cain and Able in the Wanderlust points to emotions similar to what would come from the Bible story of the two brothers of the first human family.
Anyway, the testing of such an experiment would be tricky, as it would have to be an unquestioningly loyal Agent or Doctor to not abuse the powers granted to him. Also, if we killed Able while someone was disguised as him, would the disguise fade because Able isn't technically dead, just not entirely alive? Another consideration, if the 'death' of Able ends the disguise, will his reincarnation restart it if the time hasn't expired? What about samples of flesh harvested from Ables that have 'died' before?
However, with the assertion that 034 "ONLY WORKS ON HUMANS" is contradicted quickly by saying "the results were… well… unexpected" meaning that 034 does work, just not in a manner that the Foundation likes. Further clarification should be done in the form of releasing of test files of animal/non-human species, or trying more experiments, preferably weaker easily controlled species selected.
Does the limitation of 034 apply opposite the above proposition, with animals being tried to change into others of the same species and different species, before attempting making an animal into a human form.

If the author wants to work with me, I believe it might be fun creating an experimentation log with 034. Or perhaps with his blessing I can just create one.

item.title: Re: Possible uses.
item.content:encoded: EXCEPT, all of you seemed to have overlooked the fact that SCP-034 ONLY WORKS ON HUMANS. The researchers have tried testing it on "non-humans", and the results were… well… unexpected.

item.title: Re: Possible uses.
item.content:encoded:
Its a clever and inventive idea- an army of ables, with all his ability but non of the sociopathy. We would only turn people into him that we knew were 100 percent loyal, and we could control them the same way we control the original; with an explosive collar just in case. Yes, he probably would freak out and try to kill them all if he knew, but who's to say he has to be told? And think, we can flay him all we want, but eventually he'll just regenerate, so the number of troops is limitless. Hey! Maybe that's why the guy tried to infiltrate the site! To steal skin. Makes sense.

Of course, going behind his back like that would be dangerous, but definitely interesting. I can imagine The Foundation keeping that secret from him, covering it up, hiding the different versions when he walks by.

Maybe they have a couple strips of skin stocked up just in case he goes completely rogue and somehow escapes?

This kind of ties into an idea i was thinking about. We're given the perception that the Foundation is extremely powerful, especially by the implication that they successfully capture all these keter class objects and foil their escape attempts. Like 682 for example; it's not really said exactly how it's stopped every time, but they do it. How? The Foundation doesn't have access to super science, so what sets them apart from normal soldiers and commandos? Obviously they use SCPs to their fullest advantage. I think this would be one of them.

Two things though Lacynt:

It couldn't be used on 682, it doesn't work on animals. On that line of thought, maybe it wouldn't work of able either; i mean he's not exactly human is he? didn't the article say something about his organs being different.
Secondly, not every object has to have a use or be destroyed. Thats a really bad misconception. The Foundation contains almost everything, even if it costs money or has no purpose. That's its job. If it was all about the benefits it wouldn't need funding. But its not a business or something thats completely self-sufficient. I bet governments pump money into this organization. So it doesn't have to do anything helpful. It just has to be special. I really dislike the other point of view.

item.title: Re: Possible uses.
item.content:encoded: It's already contained, and quite safely. Our job is done.

item.title: Re: Possible uses.
item.content:encoded: Temporarily… Far safer than say, trying to introduce SCP-682 to SCP-689. Could anyone imagine what would have happened if SCP-682 had adapted the ability to appear on top of any personnel that had laid eyes on it?

item.title: Re: Possible uses.
item.content:encoded: The same purpose it was being used for when found? Disguising oneself as someone else for the purpose of infiltration instead of trying to turn into a copy of a Keter-class SCP?

item.title: Re: Possible uses.
item.content:encoded: Well, how about instead of just naysaying, some people start providing alternate uses for this knife. If no use can be discovered for this obviously dangerous item, then the Foundation should begin looking towards destruction instead of storage. First cause is to protect humanity, and ourselves by proxy.

item.title: Re: Possible uses.
item.content:encoded:
Giving the power of 076-2 or 682 to someone recruited from death row. Even better.

This sort of thing sounds like a containment breach waiting to happen. I'm sure some researcher would suggest something like this at some point, but I'm equally sure said suggestion would be shot down immediately by the O5 or the senior staff. There's a fine line between "dangerous" and "reckless", and you're not supposed to cross it unless you're Dr. Kondraki.

item.title: Re: Possible uses.
item.content:encoded: The Foundation has been known to try extremely dangerous experiments with SCP items and humanoids before. This would at least help to actively reduce the number of casualties of valuable Agents. Testing would, of course, begin with D-class personnel.

item.title: Re: Possible uses.
item.content:encoded: It's just an all around bad idea.

item.title: Re: Possible uses.
item.content:encoded:
Except they would not be imitations, they would be copies. And as long as Abel realizes that it is temporary, lasting only an hour, I doubt he would destroy his own team. In fact, I believe he would enjoy the only true challenge he would ever have, himself.

How about this, SCP-682 used as the copy subject? The abilities of SCP-682, including the regenerative and adaptive abilities, with a mind for the mission. The only trouble is if SCP-682 adapts the SCP-034 ability to create copies of itself by ripping chunks of itself off. Still, it is something to think about.

item.title: Re: Possible uses.
item.content:encoded:
Except that the rest of Omega-7 would then be genuinely challenging opponents for Able, so he'd start killing them.

Besides, judging from the fate of the "Mini-Able" produced by SCP-5555 and the "L'il Able" animate figurine made by SCP-237, it seems SCP-076-2 doesn't take kindly to imitations…

item.title: Possible uses.
item.content:encoded: Provided no severe mental illnesses come forth on test subjects, I believe SCP-034 should be temporarily issued to Mobile Task Force Omega-7, and members issued bits of "Abel" (SCP-076-2) cut off with this SCP item. An entire team of "Abel's" when direly needed would reduce the amount of casualties "Pandora's Box" suffers on high-risk operations. I doubt Abel would mind too much, as he doesn't seem to register pain as horribly as normal Agents do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I especially liked the interrogation log at the end.

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded:
They do them on official reports, too. Like for major corporations and government agencies.

It's a thing that's done, that's all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's for clarification purposes, Nifar.

Also, this one is pretty cool.

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded:
It's for clarification purposes, Nifar.

Also, this one is pretty cool.

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: Ignoring the obvious repetition joke, the only people who should need that kind of clarification shouldn't be anywhere near the internet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's for clarification purposes, Nifar.

Also, this one is pretty cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's for clarification purposes, Nifar.

Also, this one is pretty cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one, but are the numbers in parenthesis really necessary? That's always bothered me on real documents when they do it, because I can never figure out why any one would need the numbers written both ways.
